results.title: AI Engineer, Global Operations | Jobspawn
highlights:
AI Engineer, Global Operations | Jobspawn

Jobspawn.← All Jobs

# AI Engineer, Global Operations

Posted Jun 1, 2026

At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain.

We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways.

Are you curious about being part of our growth stor​y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation.

Job Summary

Iron Mountain is seeking a dynamic and experienced AI Engineer to join our Operations team. In this role, you will be responsible for designing and deploying production-ready Machine Learning models and Artificial Intelligence agents that optimize complex logistics workflows and drive measurable cost reductions.

You will join a high-impact team dedicated to bridging the gap between operational business challenges and scalable technical solutions, directly influencing our supply chain and field operations.

What You’ll Do

- In this role, you will:
- Develop End-to-End AI Solutions: Design, build, and monitor production-ready Machine Learning models and automated agents to streamline multi-step operational decision-making.
- Optimize Operational Workflows: Collaborate with Finance, Facilities, and Information Technology teams to build robust Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) pipelines and integrate predictive tools into existing warehouse and logistics software.
- Govern AI Safety and Compliance: Ensure the responsible deployment of all systems by performing rigorous model validation, maintaining strict data governance, and mitigating risk or bias in automated decisions.

What You’ll Bring

- The ideal candidate will have:
- 5+ years of applied experience in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning engineering or advanced analytics, preferably within logistics, supply chain, or transportation industries.
- Advanced technical expertise in Python and SQL, with proven experience scaling "agentic" workflows and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions using platforms like LangChain or n8n.
- Proven ability in strategic problem-solving and rapid prototyping, with the communication skills necessary to explain complex model outputs to non-technical business leaders.
- A Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or Mathematics.
- United States Citizenship is required.

- What We Offer
- Competitive compensation and benefits aligned with your experience level.
- Flexible work options to support a healthy work–life balance.
- Comprehensive health, wellness, and retirement plans to support your long-term well-being.
- Opportunities for continuous learning and professional growth in a cutting-edge technical environment.

Reasonably expected salary range: $107,500.00 - $143,300.00

Please note that an employee's starting salary may vary based on a variety of factors. Where State, Municipal, Provincial, Territorial or other legal minimum wages exceed the federal minimum wage, employees are entitled to the higher rate.

Category: Information Technology

Apply for This Job

results.title: Technical Program Manager, Robotic Automation | Wfhverse
highlights: Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain. We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways. Are you curious about being part of our growth stor​y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation. Job Summary As part of Iron Mountain’s global expansion plan for our Digital Business, we are looking to bring advanced Robotic Automation solutions to cost effectively support our ambitious growth targets. The Robotic Automation Technical Program Manager will lead Iron Mountain’s entry in robotics.This is a highly visible program and marks a key milestone in our operational transformation journey. The Technical Project Manager will be responsible for the end-to-end project execution, and serve as our technical and business liaison with our Robotic Automation partners to ensure our Robotic Automation solution will meet our operational and business requirements. This role will manage the overall project scope, project metrics, budget, and schedule for the company This position offers a unique opportunity to pioneer robotic automation within our operations, laying the foundation for future robotic and digital initiatives. A successful candidate should be able to balance the various trade-offs for automation solutions and work through various challenges and setbacks to navigate the path to successful deployment and operation of the automated solutions. The other critical skill is the ability to rally the various internal and external team members so they can act as one team focusing on joint success. What You’ll Do Project Leadership & Management Lead the overall execution of the robotic project according to defined scope, cost, and schedule. Act as the main point of contact between Robotic Design firm, Manufacturing partners, Iron Mountain operations and internal support functions. Coordinate risk analysis with the integrator and manage validation tests and/or proofs of concept on critical automation steps where the technical solution is not yet fully defined. Ensure that the project achieves its performance targets in terms of time to market, capacity, quality, throughput, cost and availability. Manage project governance, including the preparation and facilitation of regular Steering Committees. Guarantee compliance with all industrial, safety, quality, and regulatory standards. Lead the discussion and planning on the details of the suitable use cases, including the phased adoption of advanced automation in Iron Mountain operation. ​ Planning & Coordination Work with internal stakeholders and customers to develop business requirements, technical and standards parameters for our process including product variation definition, technical requirements, safety requirements, compliance to necessary standards etc. Develop/review concepts to confirm the design will meet required performance metrics including cycle time, yield, uptime, CPK and Gage R&R. Consult our automation provider on mechanisms, timing, stations and machines using 3D modeling software (e.g. SolidWorks) Support fabrication, assembly, debug, and validation activities to ensure design conforms to our requirements and specifications. Develop and maintain detailed project plans, schedules, and budgets in coordination with all stakeholders across Iron

, you will bridge the gap between emerging AI capabilities and real-world business needs. Your work will directly influence how AI drives impact throughout MNTN.

’ll do

- Design and build internal AI-powered tools and integrations — including generative AI applications and autonomous AI agents — that help business teams work smarter and faster.

- Architect, develop, and maintain AI agents that automate complex multi-step workflows, make decisions, and interact with internal systems on behalf of users.

# Global SEO Content Specialist (24 months FTC)

Posted Jun 6, 2026

At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain.

We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways.

Are you curious about being part of our growth stor​y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation.

Iron Mountain is seeking a motivated Global SEO Content Specialist to join our Web and SEO team. In this role, you will be responsible for owning content performance across our website, ensuring English-language content meets Search Engine Optimisation (SEO) best practices and delivers measurable business impact.You will be a core contributor on a high-impact team that partners closely with internal product owners and global leaders to strengthen search visibility and support business growth.

What You’ll Do (Core Responsibilities)

In this role, you will:

- Execute SEO and content strategies by creating, optimizing, and maintaining content briefs and on-page updates to improve organic visibility across key solution areas.
- Collaborate with internal teams, product owners, and country leaders to identify content gaps and translate performance data into actionable recommendations that improve our web presence.
- Ensure all digital content complies with Iron Mountain brand, legal, and regulatory standards while maintaining high editorial quality through human review of Artificial Intelligence (AI) supported drafts.

What You’ll Bring (Skills & Experience)

The ideal candidate will have:

- Several years of experience in Search Engine Optimisation (SEO) and content development, preferably within a corporate or enterprise environment.
- Strong knowledge of SEO and analytics tools such as BrightEdge, Google Search Console, Google Analytics 4 (GA4), and Semrush.
- Proven ability in keyword research and performance analysis, with the skill to turn search insights into content that aligns with user intent.Professional-standard English writing and editing skills, with a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience preferred.

What We Offer (Benefits)

- Competitive compensation and benefits aligned with your experience
- Flexible work options to support a healthy work-life balance.
- Comprehensive health, wellness, and retirement plans.
- Opportunities for continuous learning and professional growth within a global organisation.

Ready to help shape the narrative of a global leader? Apply today to join Iron Mountain and help us activate the value of information for the world’s leading companies.

#LI-Remote

# Outsourced - Senior Records Analyst

Posted May 26, 2026

This a Full Remote job, the offer is available from: United States, Canada, Arizona (USA), Arkansas (USA), California (USA), Florida (USA), New York (USA)

At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain.

We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways.

Are you curious about being part of our growth stor​y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation.

Iron Mountain is seeking a highly motivated Outsourced Senior Records Analyst to join our Records & Information Management team. This is a critical remote role responsible for ensuring the compliant, efficient, and secure management of our client's records .

### What You'll Do (Responsibilities):

- In this role, you will:
- Lead the development, implementation, and maintenance of records retention schedules and classification systems, ensuring they align with business needs
- Collaborate with business units and stakeholders to provide proactive communication, manage service levels, and deliver specialized training on retention schedules and Iron Mountain systems.
- Conduct regular, proactive audits and systems analysis on electronic and physical records management programs to assess compliance, identify risks, and implement corrective actions to improve accuracy and efficiency across the organization.

### What You'll Bring (Skills & Qualifications)

- The ideal candidate will have:
- 2-4 years of experience in Records & Information Management or a related field
- Strong knowledge of records management principles, including lifecycle management, retention scheduling, and critical regulatory compliance (e.g., HIPAA, GxP, or similar data/privacy regulations).
- Proven ability in analytical problem-solving and technical systems expertise (e.g., using Electronic Document/Records Management Systems or DMS) to manage complex data sets and ensure high data quality.
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience; professional certifications such as CRA, CRM, CIP, or IGP are highly preferred

- What We Offer (Benefits) :
- Starting Pay Rate : $75,000
- Annual Bonus : 5%
- Comprehensive health, wellness, and retirement plans.
- Flexible annual leaves
- Remote work option to support work–life balance.
- Opportunities for continuous learning and professional growth.

Are you ready to elevate your career? Apply today to join Iron Mountain and directly contribute to achieving a significant impact on compliance and data integrity!

Reasonably expected salary range: $68,400.00 - $85,500.00

Please note that an employee's starting salary may vary based on a variety of factors. Where State, Municipal, Provincial, Territorial or other legal minimum wages exceed the federal minimum wage, employees are entitled to the higher rate.

# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
[...]
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Senior Product Manager – SaaS Platform Architecture & Experience | Hirevector

# Senior Product Manager – SaaS Platform Architecture & Experience

Posted May 30, 2026

This a Full Remote job, the offer is available from: Florida (USA)

At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain.

We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways.

Are you curious about being part of our growth stor​y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation.

Senior Product Manager – SaaS Platform Architecture & Experience

Travel Requirements: 20% including international

### Summary

We are seeking a Senior Product Manager – SaaS Platform Architecture & Experience to lead the evolution of Insight DXP into a world-class, enterprise-grade platform.

This is a "Platform-as-a-Product" role. You are the architect of the internal and external developer experience. You will be responsible for turning complex technical components into a seamless, self-service management console while ensuring the underlying infrastructure is secure, scalable, and high-performance. You bridge the gap between "it works" and "it’s a world-class product," ensuring our partners and vertical teams can build at speed on a bulletproof foundation.

- In this role you will be:
- Leading the "Self-Service" Strategy: Transforming internal technical tools into an intuitive, no-code Management Console that allows partners and clients to configure their own back-office workflows.
- Guarding Platform Integrity: Owning the roadmap for enterprise-grade requirements, including scalability, sub-second latency, and global security compliance (SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR).
- Defining the Developer Experience (DX): Overseeing the API strategy, webhooks, and documentation to ensure the platform is highly interoperable with external enterprise systems (ERPs, CRMs).
- Driving Platform Velocity: Collaborating with Engineering to build reusable "Lego blocks" that allow our internal Solutions PMs to launch new industry plays (Claims, Loans, etc.) in record time.
- Proving Platform Value: Developing internal analytics dashboards that track processing costs, ROI, and system health for executive-level reporting.

- What you will need to succeed:
- BS required (Engineering or CS preferred); MBA highly desired.
- 3–5 years of experience as a PM for a global SaaS platform, with a focus on Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) or infrastructure.
- Proven experience with end-to-end product delivery, specifically managing the trade-offs between feature velocity and system hardening.
- Demonstrated ability to own a technical roadmap and advocate for infrastructure excellence in a fast-paced environment.
- Experience with Developer-centric platforms and a deep understanding of API-first product strategies.

Category: Marketing

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

# lululemon Applied AI/ML Scientist
[...]
Description & Requirements Who we are lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. That includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. About this team The Enterprise Data & AI team is a strategic and operational driver of growth for lululemon, owning and building the data and AI platforms and products that enable the enterprise to operate with intelligence at scale. The team leads the design and delivery of a trusted unified data foundation, advanced analytics capabilities, and AI solutions across lululemon's vertically integrated retail ecosystem, embedding strong data governance and responsible AI practices from the very beginning. By applying AI to critical business challenges and creating new, transformative AI solutions, the team helps reshape how lululemon operates. Through deep partnership with product, technology, and business teams, Enterprise Data & AI accelerates product innovation, unlocks measurable value, elevates guest and educator experiences, and drives enterprise efficiency. Core responsibilities As an Applied AI/ML Scientist, you design, develop, test, and optimize AI/ML solutions that address defined business problems, with an emphasis on applied modeling, experimentation, and translating advanced AI research into scalable, production-ready solutions. You work independently to design and adapt AI/ML approaches to build prototypes, and improve model performance, reliability, and efficiency. You use domain knowledge and collaborate with stakeholders and product teams to map the business problem to the right model and AI/ML solution approach. You own the AI solution implementation and partner with engineering teams to integrate solutions into the broader engineering system. You contribute to solution enhancements, validate outcomes, and continuously improve models based on real world performance. Examples of projects could be
[...]
- Designing and deploying machine learning and predictive/generative AI models for demand forecasting, optimization, personalization and search, intelligent automation, and multimodal content creation and enrichment
[...]
- Applying NLP, computer vision, multimodal, or time series modeling techniques to enterprise-scale datasets that are a mix of structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Developing experimentation frameworks and evaluation approaches to measure business impact of AI solutions in production
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, machine learning, mathematics, or related field, Masters preferred
[...]
- 4-8 years of experience building and optimizing AI/ML solutions, or equivalent research experience, including educational experience (e.g., Master's or PhD degree)
[...]
- Demonstrated experience in evaluating and applying state-of-the-art AI/ML models and methods to develop practical, high-performing solutions to specific use-cases.
[...]
- Experience designing AI/ML approaches, conducting experiments to evaluate performance, iterating on solution design to improve performance, and translating AI-ML driven insights into business goals

- Experience with ML implementation using commonly used tools such as Python, pyTorch, cloud ML platforms and libraries, open-source toolkits.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.

Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.

## Building an AI Center of Excellence: Lessons from Iron Mountain's Journey
[...]
By: Swami Jayaraman July 29th, 2025
[...]
Why We Created Our AI Center of Excellence (CoE) When ChatGPT burst onto the scene at the end of 2022, it was uncharted waters for many. Everybody was trying to figure out what their AI strategy should be. At Iron Mountain, we acknowledged that we don't know what we don't know, and we needed a diverse team of experts to help us understand and implement AI—not just technology experts, but legal experts, compliance experts, and people who know how the work gets done in our business units.
